description: Phase 1 of 3 of the Strata web console — embedded read-only operator UI modelled on MinIO Console (single-binary embed, same auth) and CockroachDB DB Console (cluster-overview home page with status, node count, per-node health, version, uptime). Tech: React 18 + Vite + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui + TanStack Query (5 s polling) + recharts + Playwright e2e. Bundle embedded into cmd/strata via go:embed and served from /console/* on the gateway port. Auth: session-cookie + JWT against existing IAM credentials (Cassandra/TiKV access_keys table). New /admin/v1/* JSON HTTP API + OpenAPI 3.1 spec is the contract between UI and gateway. Pages: Login, Cluster Overview (home — CockroachDB-shape hero + nodes table + top-buckets widget + top-consumers widget), Buckets list, Bucket detail (read-only object browser), Metrics dashboard (request rate, p50/p95/p99 latency, error rate, bytes — 15m/1h/6h/24h/7d windows). Heartbeat table cluster_nodes (10 s write, 30 s TTL) lockstep across memory + cassandra + tikv backends. No write actions in Phase 1 (Phase 2 prd-web-ui-admin.md). No debug tooling in Phase 1 (Phase 3 prd-web-ui-debug.md). Bundle target ≤500 KiB gzipped, time-to-interactive ≤500 ms on local stack.

userStories.title: Vite + React + TypeScript skeleton + go:embed
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a minimal React+TS app under web/ that compiles to a static bundle and is embedded into cmd/strata via go:embed, so subsequent stories can fill in real pages without re-litigating the build pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: New web/ source tree (sibling of cmd/, internal/, docs/) with web/package.json, web/vite.config.ts, web/tsconfig.json, web/index.html, web/src/main.tsx, web/src/App.tsx (renders 'Strata Console — coming soon')
Pinned versions: react@18.x, react-dom@18.x, vite@5.x, typescript@5.x. Lockfile checked in (web/pnpm-lock.yaml — pnpm preferred over npm/yarn for size + reproducibility)
make web-build runs cd web && pnpm install && pnpm run build and produces web/dist/ (index.html + assets/*)

cmd/strata/console.go (new file) declares //go:embed all:web/dist + var consoleFS embed.FS and exposes a handler consoleHandler() that serves the embedded files at /console/* (with SPA fallback to index.html for client-side routes)

internal/serverapp/serverapp.go registers the handler under /console/ ahead of the catch-all / S3 router so the UI does not collide with bucket names. Verify bucket name 'console' is rejected by the existing validBucketName validator (add to it if not)
make build runs make web-build first; missing web/dist fails the Go build cleanly with a helpful error
CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml gains a web-build step using node@20.x + pnpm@9.x; cached ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
make run-memory boots; curl http://localhost:9000/console/ returns index.html; the page renders 'Strata Console — coming soon' without console errors
Typecheck passes (go vet + pnpm run typecheck)
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented in commit e4094bf (web/ scaffold + root strata package console.go + ConsoleHandler + SPA fallback + /console/ wired in cmd/strata-gateway/main.go). Variance from spec: console.go lives at repo root (package strata) not cmd/strata/console.go because the gateway binary is cmd/strata-gateway/, not cmd/strata/. make run-memory uses :9999 not :9000. Build/typecheck/tests pass; curl /console/ returns 200 HTML, asset path 200, SPA deep-link fallback 200. Bundle 46 KiB gzipped (target ≤500 KiB).

userStories.title: Design system — Tailwind + shadcn/ui + dark mode
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui (radix primitives + design tokens) wired up so subsequent stories use a consistent component vocabulary without per-page styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Tailwind 3.x + PostCSS + autoprefixer; web/tailwind.config.ts configures content scan, font stack (system-ui), and CSS variables for theme tokens

Install shadcn/ui CLI; copy in baseline components: Button, Card, Input, Label, Tabs, Dialog, Sheet, DropdownMenu, Table, Select, Toast, Skeleton, Badge. Each lives under web/src/components/ui/<name>.tsx

Design tokens in web/src/styles/globals.css: neutral grayscale foundation, primary = blue (hsl(220 90% 56%)), destructive = red (hsl(0 72% 50%)), success = green, warning = amber. Light + dark variants
Dark mode toggle in the top bar (dropdown: Light / Dark / System default = System); persisted to localStorage['strata.theme']; class='dark' on <html> driven by the toggle
Typography scale: text-xs 12 / text-sm 14 / text-base 16 / text-lg 18 / text-xl 20 / text-2xl 24 (Tailwind defaults)
No custom logo or brand mark in this story (out of scope; keep placeholder 'Strata Console' wordmark)
Theme toggle flips <html class='dark'> on/off; tokens render correctly in both themes (Playwright snapshot screenshot pair)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented this iteration. Tailwind 3.4.17 + PostCSS 8.4 + autoprefixer 10.4 + tailwindcss-animate 1.0.7. Path alias '@/*' wired in vite.config.ts + tsconfig.app.json. Design tokens light+dark in web/src/styles/globals.css (primary 220 90% 56%, destructive 0 72% 50%, success 142 71% 40%, warning 38 92% 50%). All 13 baseline shadcn components copied as source under web/src/components/ui/ (Button, Card, Input, Label, Tabs, Dialog, Sheet, DropdownMenu, Table, Select, Toast, Skeleton, Badge). ThemeProvider in web/src/components/theme-provider.tsx (Light/Dark/System, system tracks prefers-color-scheme media query, toggles class='dark' on <html>, persists to localStorage['strata.theme']). ThemeToggle in top bar uses DropdownMenu. Bundle: 81 KiB gzipped JS + 5.5 KiB gzipped CSS (was 46 KiB at US-001; budget ~250 KiB headroom remaining). Verified: pnpm typecheck clean, pnpm build clean, go vet clean, go test ./... clean, curl /console/ + asset + SPA-fallback all 200. Browser snapshot verification deferred to US-012 Playwright (no browser MCP in this iteration). Manual: load /console/ in a browser; click theme toggle in the top bar; verify light/dark/system flips <html class='dark'> on/off.

userStories.title: /admin/v1/* HTTP API scaffolding (Go side)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a versioned /admin/v1/* JSON HTTP namespace on the gateway, with a stub handler tree that subsequent stories fill in, so the UI has a stable contract to call.
acceptanceCriteria: New package internal/adminapi/ with Server struct holding the meta.Store + Prometheus client + Logger + auth verifier

Routes registered under /admin/v1/ ahead of the S3 router (same prefix-routing pattern as /console/): GET /admin/v1/cluster/status (stub: {status: 'ok', version: '<git sha>', started_at: <epoch>}), GET /admin/v1/cluster/nodes (stub: {nodes: []}), GET /admin/v1/buckets (stub: {buckets: []}), GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket} (stub: 404), GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects (stub: {objects: []}), GET /admin/v1/consumers/top (stub: {consumers: []}), GET /admin/v1/metrics/timeseries (stub: {series: []})
All endpoints return JSON; Content-Type: application/json
All endpoints require authentication via the same SigV4 path the S3 API uses OR a session cookie (US-004 wires the cookie path); anonymous requests return 401 Unauthorized
OpenAPI 3.1 spec checked in at internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml describing all endpoints with request/response schemas — used by Phase 2/3 for typegen + as a contract document
Per-endpoint unit tests under internal/adminapi/*_test.go asserting status codes + JSON shapes (stub data is fine; real data lands in later stories)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented this iteration. New package internal/adminapi (server.go + cluster.go + buckets.go + consumers.go + metrics.go + types.go + openapi.yaml). Server holds Meta + CredentialsStore + Version (vcs.revision via runtime/debug.ReadBuildInfo, override STRATA_VERSION) + Started time. Handler() wraps an inner http.ServeMux (go 1.22+ method-prefixed patterns) with auth.Middleware{Mode: ModeRequired} so /admin/v1/* always demands auth regardless of global STRATA_AUTH_MODE. Anonymous → 401 JSON {code, message}. JSON deny handler writeAuthDenied (NOT s3api.WriteAuthDenied which emits XML). Routes registered: GET /admin/v1/cluster/status (stub status:'ok' + version + started_at), GET /admin/v1/cluster/nodes ({nodes:[]}), GET /admin/v1/buckets ({buckets:[], total:0}), GET /admin/v1/buckets/top ({buckets:[]}), GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket} (404 NoSuchBucket), GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects ({objects:[], common_prefixes:[], is_truncated:false}), GET /admin/v1/consumers/top ({consumers:[]}), GET /admin/v1/metrics/timeseries ({series:[]}). Wired in cmd/strata-gateway/main.go: mux.Handle('/admin/v1/', adminServer.Handler()) ahead of the catch-all '/' S3 router. OpenAPI 3.1 spec at internal/adminapi/openapi.yaml documents all endpoints + schemas + sigv4/sessionCookie security schemes (cookie auth lands US-004). Tests in internal/adminapi/server_test.go: 401 anonymous via Handler(); per-endpoint 200/404 + JSON-shape via routes() (auth-bypass for shape assertion). go vet clean, go test ./... clean, go build ./... clean, pnpm typecheck clean. Runtime smoke on :9998: curl /admin/v1/cluster/status anon → 401 JSON; /admin/v1/cluster/nodes anon → 401 JSON; /admin/v1/buckets/foo anon → 401 JSON; /console/ → 200 (regression-clean).

userStories.title: Auth — login form, session cookies, JWT
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to log in to the console with my existing IAM access key + secret, get a session cookie, and have all subsequent /admin/v1/* calls authenticated without re-prompting until 24 h pass.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint POST /admin/v1/auth/login accepts JSON {access_key, secret_key}; verifies against existing IAM credentials store (internal/auth.Store); on success returns Set-Cookie: strata_session=<jwt>; HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict; Path=/admin; Max-Age=86400

JWT payload: {sub: <access-key>, iat, exp}; signed with HS256 using a per-deployment secret read from STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET (random 32-byte hex; if unset, generated at startup with a WARN log line — production deployments MUST set it explicitly to survive restarts without invalidating sessions)

Auth middleware on /admin/v1/* (except /admin/v1/auth/login): reads strata_session cookie, validates JWT, sets auth.AuthInfo{AccessKey: <sub>} on the request context. Falls back to SigV4 path for clients that don't carry a cookie
POST /admin/v1/auth/logout clears the cookie (Max-Age=0); always returns 200
GET /admin/v1/auth/whoami returns {access_key, expires_at} for the active session; unauthenticated returns 401

React login page at /console/login: form with Access Key + Secret Key inputs (Secret Key is type=password), submits to /admin/v1/auth/login. On success: redirect to /console/ (cluster overview). On failure: inline error message
React <RequireAuth> wrapper redirects unauthenticated routes to /console/login; uses whoami probe on mount
React <UserMenu> in the top bar shows the access key + a 'Sign out' item; click triggers POST /admin/v1/auth/logout + redirect to /console/login
Login with valid creds → 200 + cookie set + redirect to /console/; invalid creds → inline error + still on /console/login; logout → cookie cleared, redirect to /console/login
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented this iteration. Go side: internal/adminapi/session.go (HS256 JWT sign/verify, no external dep — stdlib crypto/hmac + sha256 + base64.RawURLEncoding) + internal/adminapi/auth.go (handleLogin / handleLogout / handleWhoami) + internal/adminapi/server.go rewrite of authMiddleware (cookie → SigV4 fallback; bypass for /admin/v1/auth/login + /auth/logout). adminapi.New now takes a jwtSecret []byte. cmd/strata-gateway/main.go loads STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET (hex 32B) or generates ephemeral secret with WARN. Cookie shape per spec: HttpOnly + SameSite=Strict + Path=/admin + Max-Age=86400; Secure flag conditional on r.TLS != nil OR X-Forwarded-Proto=https so dev over plain HTTP works. Login subtle.ConstantTimeCompare on the secret; constant-time fake-compare on the unknown-access-key branch to avoid leaking key existence. /auth/whoami requires auth (returns AccessKey + ExpiresAt from cookie). OpenAPI 3.1 spec updated with three new operations + LoginRequest/SessionInfo schemas. Tests in internal/adminapi/auth_test.go: TestLoginSuccess (cookie attrs verified), TestLoginInvalidSecret, TestLoginUnknownAccessKey, TestLoginMalformedBody (400), TestLogoutClearsCookie, TestWhoamiUnauthenticated (401), TestWhoamiWithSessionCookie, TestSessionCookieGrantsAccess (login → cookie → /cluster/status 200), TestForgedSessionRejected, TestExpiredSessionRejected, TestVerifySessionRoundTrip. React side: web/src/api/auth.ts (login/logout/whoami fetch wrappers + AuthError class) + web/src/components/auth-provider.tsx (AuthProvider context with state machine 'unknown' | 'unauthenticated' | 'authenticated'; whoami probe on mount) + web/src/components/require-auth.tsx (Navigate to /login on unauth) + web/src/components/user-menu.tsx (DropdownMenu with access key + Sign out) + web/src/pages/Login.tsx (form Card; AccessKey + SecretKey inputs; inline error on failure). react-router-dom@6.28 added; main.tsx wraps in BrowserRouter basename='/console' + AuthProvider. Quality: go vet/test/build clean (adminapi tests now 19 — 8 prior + 11 new). pnpm typecheck/build clean — bundle now 277.4 KiB raw / 90.6 KiB gzipped JS + 27.5 KiB raw / 5.5 KiB gzipped CSS (was 81 KiB gzipped at US-002; +9.6 KiB for react-router + auth UI; budget ≤500 KiB ✅). Runtime smoke on :9997 with STRATA_AUTH_MODE=required + STRATA_STATIC_CREDENTIALS=test:test:owner + STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32): /admin/v1/auth/whoami anon → 401; /admin/v1/auth/login wrong secret → 401 InvalidCredentials; /admin/v1/auth/login correct → 200 + Set-Cookie strata_session=...; /admin/v1/auth/whoami w/ cookie → 200 {access_key,expires_at}; /admin/v1/cluster/status w/ cookie → 200; /admin/v1/auth/logout → 200 + Max-Age=0 clears cookie; /admin/v1/auth/whoami after logout → 401. Browser MCP not available this iteration; full browser run scheduled for US-012 Playwright.

userStories.title: App layout — sidebar + top bar + content area
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the standard CockroachDB / MinIO console layout — left sidebar with primary nav, top bar with cluster name + user menu + theme toggle + global search, content area for the active route — so navigation feels native.
acceptanceCriteria: Layout component at web/src/components/layout/AppShell.tsx: left sidebar (collapsible to icon-only on viewport <1024 px) with primary nav items: Overview (home), Buckets, Consumers, Metrics, Settings (placeholder for Phase 2). Top bar: cluster name (from /admin/v1/cluster/status::cluster_name), global search input (placeholder for Phase 2), theme toggle, user menu (US-004). Content area: routed via react-router-dom v6.x <Outlet>
Active nav item is highlighted; clicking navigates without page reload (SPA)
Sidebar collapse state persisted to localStorage['strata.sidebar.collapsed']
Mobile (viewport <640 px): sidebar becomes a Sheet (slide-out drawer) triggered by a hamburger button in the top bar
Nav between Overview and Buckets pages keeps the layout stable; sidebar collapse persists across reloads
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented this iteration. Go side: ClusterStatus type gained ClusterName (json: cluster_name); /admin/v1/cluster/status now returns it from Server.ClusterName, sourced from STRATA_CLUSTER_NAME env (default 'strata' when empty). adminapi.New signature gained clusterName arg; main.go passes os.Getenv('STRATA_CLUSTER_NAME'). server_test.go + auth_test.go updated to pass 'test-cluster'; TestClusterStatusShape now asserts the new field. React side: web/src/api/cluster.ts (fetchClusterStatus + ClusterStatus type with optional fields for US-006 expansion). web/src/components/layout/{nav.ts,SidebarNav.tsx,AppShell.tsx}: AppShell holds the persistent sidebar (sticky, w-60 expanded / w-14 collapsed) + top bar (cluster name from /cluster/status with 'Strata' fallback, Phase 1 badge, disabled global search input placeholder, ThemeToggle, UserMenu) + main content via <Outlet>. Sidebar nav (Overview, Buckets, Consumers, Metrics, Settings) uses NavLink → active state via aria-current/className. Collapse button on the sidebar header toggles useState + persists localStorage['strata.sidebar.collapsed']='1'|'0'. useMediaQuery hook tracks (max-width: 1023px) and (max-width: 639px). Narrow viewport (<1024px) forces icon-only sidebar regardless of persisted preference; the manual toggle is hidden in that range. Mobile (<640px) hides the desktop sidebar entirely and shows a hamburger Menu button in the top bar that opens a Sheet (slide-out drawer, side='left') containing the same SidebarNav with onNavigate=close-sheet. Stub pages: web/src/pages/{Overview,Buckets,Consumers,Metrics,Settings}.tsx — each renders a header + Card 'coming soon' placeholder so navigation is testable. App.tsx restructured: /login standalone; nested routes ('/', '/buckets', '/consumers', '/metrics', '/settings', '*' → /) inside <Route element={<RequireAuth><AppShell /></RequireAuth>}>. HomeShell deleted (replaced by AppShell + OverviewPage). Quality: go vet clean, go test ./... clean (root + adminapi + auth + leader + lifecycle + meta/memory + s3api), go build ./... clean, pnpm typecheck clean, pnpm build clean — bundle 96.4 KiB gzipped JS + 5.9 KiB gzipped CSS (was 90.6 KiB JS at US-004; +5.8 KiB for AppShell + 5 stub pages). Runtime smoke on :9996 with STRATA_CLUSTER_NAME=ralph-shell + STRATA_AUTH_MODE=required + STRATA_STATIC_CREDENTIALS=test:test:owner: /console/ → 200, /admin/v1/auth/login (test:test) → 200 + Set-Cookie, /admin/v1/cluster/status w/ cookie → {status:'ok',version:'dev',started_at:...,cluster_name:'ralph-shell'}, /console/buckets SPA fallback → 200. Browser MCP unavailable; full Playwright run scheduled for US-012.

userStories.title: Cluster Overview home page (CockroachDB-shape)
userStories.description: As an operator opening the console, I want the home page to show cluster health at a glance — overall status, node count, per-node health, build version, uptime — modelled on the CockroachDB DB Console's overview page.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/cluster/status returns: {cluster_name (from STRATA_CLUSTER_NAME or 'strata'), version (git sha + tag), started_at (epoch-ms), uptime_sec, status: 'healthy' | 'degraded' | 'unhealthy', node_count, node_count_healthy, meta_backend: 'cassandra' | 'tikv' | 'memory', data_backend: 'rados' | 's3' | 'memory'}. status derived: healthy if all nodes report /readyz 200 in last 30 s; degraded if 1 or more failing but quorum holds; unhealthy if quorum lost

GET /admin/v1/cluster/nodes returns: {nodes: [{id (hostname or pod name), address (host:port), version, started_at, uptime_sec, status, workers ['gc','lifecycle',...], leader_for ['gc-leader',...]}]}. Source: every Strata replica writes a heartbeat row to cluster_nodes table (memory + cassandra + tikv in lockstep) every 10 s with TTL 30 s. Replica reads the table to assemble the response. Leadership info comes from the existing internal/leader lease tables (one row per worker)

Cassandra: ALTER/CREATE TABLE cluster_nodes (node_id text PRIMARY KEY, address text, version text, started_at timestamp, workers set<text>, leader_for set<text>, last_heartbeat timestamp) WITH default_time_to_live = 30. TiKV: same shape encoded under s/cluster_nodes/<node_id> with TTL applied via a background sweeper goroutine. Memory: in-process map with timestamp expiry.

Cluster Overview page at /console/: Hero card (cluster status badge green/amber/red, cluster name, version, uptime '3 days, 12 hours', node-count summary '4 of 5 nodes healthy'). Nodes table (id, address, version, uptime, status badge, workers chips, leader-for chips. Sortable by status (unhealthy first), then id. Click-through to a node-detail panel placeholder for Phase 2; for now click is a no-op). Backend chips (meta + data) under the hero ('Cassandra' + 'RADOS')
Polling: 5 s via TanStack Query (US-008 sets up the global QueryClient if not yet)

Empty state: when nodes is empty (single-replica dev stack without heartbeats yet) the page still renders with the local node from cluster/status and an info banner 'Heartbeat table empty — running single-replica or just started'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Implemented this iteration. Heartbeat infra: new internal/heartbeat package (heartbeat.go + memory.go + cassandra.go + heartbeat_test.go with 4 tests). Node + Store interface + Heartbeater goroutine. Defaults: Interval 10 s, TTL 30 s. Memory store filters expired rows at read time; cassandra store passes USING TTL 30 to INSERTs. cluster_nodes DDL added to internal/meta/cassandra/schema.go tableDDL with default_time_to_live=30 (existing ensureTables bootstrap creates it). NOTE: TiKV backend mentioned in PRD does not exist in the codebase — heartbeat scope = memory + cassandra only. adminapi.New refactored to take adminapi.Config struct (Meta, Creds, Heartbeat, Version, ClusterName, MetaBackend, DataBackend, JWTSecret). ClusterStatus extended with uptime_sec + node_count + node_count_healthy + meta_backend + data_backend. ClusterNode extended with last_heartbeat. Status enum tightened to healthy/degraded/unhealthy (legacy 'ok' stub gone). handleClusterStatus aggregates Heartbeat.ListNodes (healthy iff all listed nodes fresh and ≥1; unhealthy iff empty/error). handleClusterNodes returns live heartbeat rows mapped to JSON; per-node status derived from heartbeat age (≤10 s healthy, ≤30 s degraded). cmd/strata-gateway/main.go: buildHeartbeatStore(cfg, metaStore) switches on cfg.MetaBackend; Heartbeater goroutine starts post-mux setup tied to SIGTERM cancellation. React side: web/src/api/cluster.ts gained ClusterNode + ClusterNodesResponse types + fetchClusterNodes wrapper. web/src/pages/Overview.tsx rewritten end-to-end: useClusterOverview hook polls Promise.all([fetchClusterStatus, fetchClusterNodes]) every 5 s; HeroCard (cluster name + status badge + 'X of Y nodes healthy' + 4-up grid version/uptime/nodes/started); BackendChips (Database icon for meta, Server icon for data); NodesTable (sortable: unhealthy first then degraded then healthy then by id; cells: node, address, version, uptime, status badge, workers chips, leader_for chips); empty-state info banner when nodes==[]; error banner when fetch fails; skeleton rows during initial load. Polling cadence is plain useEffect+setInterval — US-008 will swap for TanStack Query useQuery. Workers + LeaderFor populated as empty arrays for the gateway (Phase 2 hook for gc/lifecycle daemons to surface their workers + leader_for). Quality: go vet/test/build clean (heartbeat 4 tests; adminapi 21 tests). pnpm typecheck/build clean — bundle 98.6 KiB gzipped JS + 6.4 KiB gzipped CSS (was 96.4 KiB JS at US-005; +2.2 KiB for Overview page; budget ≤500 KiB). Runtime smoke on :9995 with STRATA_AUTH_MODE=required + STRATA_STATIC_CREDENTIALS=test:test:owner + STRATA_CLUSTER_NAME=ralph-overview + STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32): /admin/v1/auth/login → 200 + Set-Cookie; /admin/v1/cluster/status w/ cookie → {status:'healthy',version:'dev',started_at:...,uptime_sec:81,cluster_name:'ralph-overview',node_count:1,node_count_healthy:1,meta_backend:'memory',data_backend:'memory'}; /admin/v1/cluster/nodes w/ cookie → {nodes:[{id:'<host>',address:':9995',version:'dev',started_at:...,uptime_sec:81,status:'healthy',workers:[],leader_for:[],last_heartbeat:...}]}; /console/ → 200 (regression-clean). Browser MCP unavailable; full Playwright run scheduled for US-012.

userStories.title: Top Buckets + Top Consumers widgets on home
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the home page to also show the top 10 buckets (by stored bytes and 24h request count) and top 10 consumers (by 24h request count and bytes) so I see who is active without leaving the overview.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets/top?by=size|requests&limit=10 returns {buckets: [{name, size_bytes, object_count, request_count_24h}]}. Source: bucketstats package (already exists for Prometheus exposition) + Prometheus query for 24h request count

GET /admin/v1/consumers/top?by=requests|bytes&limit=10 returns {consumers: [{access_key, user, request_count_24h, bytes_24h}]}. Source: Prometheus strata_http_request_total{access_key=...} aggregation over 24h via PromQL

Two widgets on the Cluster Overview page below the nodes table: Top Buckets card with a tabbed view (By Size / By Requests), each tab a small table (rank, name, value, sparkline of 24h activity). Click bucket name → /console/buckets/<name>. Top Consumers card with the same tabbed shape (By Requests / By Bytes), each tab a table (rank, access key truncated 8 chars, user, value)
Empty state when Prometheus is unreachable: render '—' in value cells + a small 'Metrics unavailable' warning under the card title (do NOT crash the page)
Widgets render; tab-switch works; Prometheus-down case shows the warning
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: TanStack Query setup + 5-second polling
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single TanStack Query QueryClient configured for the whole app with sensible defaults (5 s stale time, 5 s refetch interval, retry 1) so individual components don't re-implement polling.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @tanstack/react-query@5.x + @tanstack/react-query-devtools@5.x (devtools only in dev mode)
web/src/lib/query.ts exports a configured queryClient with: staleTime: 5_000, refetchInterval: 5_000, refetchOnWindowFocus: true, retry: 1
<QueryClientProvider> wraps <App> in web/src/main.tsx

web/src/api/client.ts provides typed wrappers (fetchClusterStatus, fetchClusterNodes, fetchTopBuckets(by), fetchTopConsumers(by), fetchBucketsList(query), fetchBucket(name), fetchObjects(bucket, prefix, marker), fetchMetricsTimeseries(metric, range)) — each calls /admin/v1/* and parses the JSON
Pages use useQuery({queryKey, queryFn}) — polling is handled by the global refetchInterval
Network errors render an inline <Toast> 'Failed to load <X>' with a Retry button; the page does NOT blank-out on intermittent network errors
Cluster Overview page auto-refreshes every 5 s (mock the API to return changing uptime_sec; assert it ticks)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Cluster metrics dashboard
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a Metrics page showing request rate, p50/p95/p99 latency, and error rate over a selectable time window (15 min / 1 h / 6 h / 24 h / 7 d) so I can answer 'is the cluster healthy right now?' without leaving the console for Grafana.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/metrics/timeseries?metric=<name>&range=<duration>&step=<duration> returns {series: [{name, points: [[<epoch-ms>, <float>]]}]}. Source: in-process Prometheus client; for queries we go via PromQL against a configured Prometheus URL. Endpoint gracefully degrades to 'Metrics unavailable' payload when STRATA_PROMETHEUS_URL is unset
Supported metrics in metric param: request_rate, latency_p50, latency_p95, latency_p99, error_rate, bytes_in, bytes_out

React /console/metrics page: time range selector (segmented control: 15m / 1h / 6h / 24h / 7d). Four charts in a 2×2 grid: Request rate (req/s), Latency (p50/p95/p99 layered), Error rate (% of 5xx), Bytes (in/out layered). Use recharts 2.x — small bundle, declarative. Hover tooltips show timestamp + value; legend below each chart
Auto-refresh every 5 s for ranges ≤6 h; every 30 s for 24h+; every 5 min for 7d (controlled per-query refetchInterval)
Empty state when Prometheus is unreachable: render skeleton + 'Metrics unavailable — set STRATA_PROMETHEUS_URL' inline message
Navigate to /console/metrics, assert all 4 charts render with axis labels + at least one data point; switch range to 24h, assert request fires with range=24h

userStories.title: Buckets list page
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a Buckets page listing every bucket with search, sort, and basic info (name, owner, region, created, size, object count) so I can quickly find a bucket without aws-cli.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets?query=<substring>&sort=<col>&order=<asc|desc>&page=<n>&page_size=<n> returns {buckets: [{name, owner, region, created_at, size_bytes, object_count}], total}. Source: meta.Store::ListBuckets + bucketstats

React /console/buckets page: top toolbar (search input debounce 300 ms, refresh button). Table columns: Name (link), Owner, Region, Created, Size, Object Count. Click column header to sort. Default sort: created DESC. Pagination: 50 rows per page, page numbers + prev/next. Empty state: 'No buckets' + (Phase 2) 'Create your first bucket' button (placeholder, not wired). Click bucket name → /console/buckets/<name> (US-011)
Search filters by case-insensitive substring match on the name column server-side
List paginates, sort flips order, search filters

userStories.title: Bucket detail page (read-only object browser)
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a bucket detail page showing the bucket's metadata + a read-only object browser (folder navigation, sort, basic info per object) so I can inspect contents without aws-cli.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket} returns {name, owner, region, created_at, versioning: 'Enabled' | 'Suspended' | 'Off', object_lock: bool, size_bytes, object_count}. 404 when bucket missing

GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects?prefix=<>&marker=<>&delimiter=/&page_size=<> returns {objects: [{key, size, last_modified, etag, storage_class}], common_prefixes: [], next_marker, is_truncated}. Source: gateway's existing ListObjects handler reused via meta.Store::ScanObjects (or ListObjects fan-out) — same shape as the S3 wire response, JSON-encoded

React /console/buckets/<name> page: header (bucket name + status badges Versioning / Object-Lock). Stats bar (size, object count, region, created). Object browser: breadcrumb ('/' → 'logs/' → 'logs/2026/'), filter input (prefix prefix; debounce 300 ms). Table columns (Name link/folder icon, Size, Last Modified, Storage Class, ETag truncated 8 chars). Click folder drills in (updates URL ?prefix=...); click file → object detail panel slide-in (Sheet) showing full metadata. Phase 2 adds Download / Delete buttons; Phase 1 leaves them disabled (greyed out with 'Coming soon' tooltip). Pagination: continuation tokens (next page button); 100 rows per page
Empty bucket: 'This bucket is empty' empty state
Bucket detail loads, object list paginates, prefix navigation works, object detail panel opens

userStories.title: Playwright e2e + critical-path tests + docs/ui.md + ROADMAP
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Playwright e2e covering the critical paths (login → cluster overview → buckets list → bucket detail → logout) running on every PR, an operator guide in docs/ui.md, and the ROADMAP entry flipped to Done.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @playwright/test@1.x in web/; new web/playwright.config.ts with: chromium-only, baseURL http://localhost:9000, webServer running make run-memory (memory backend; no Cassandra/RADOS dependency for e2e)

web/e2e/critical-path.spec.ts covers: anonymous load → redirect to /console/login. Login with seed creds (set via STRATA_STATIC_CREDENTIALS=test:test:owner in webServer env) → land on /console/. Cluster Overview shows '1 of 1 nodes healthy' hero. Click Buckets nav → list page (empty state initially). Use the harness API or aws-cli to create a bucket; refresh; bucket appears in list. Click bucket → bucket detail loads. Logout → cookie cleared, back on login page
CI workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml gains an e2e-ui job after the existing e2e job; uploads the Playwright report web/playwright-report/ as e2e-ui-report artefact on failure

New docs/ui.md operator guide: when to use the console vs aws-cli (decision matrix); required env vars: STRATA_CONSOLE_JWT_SECRET, STRATA_CLUSTER_NAME, STRATA_PROMETHEUS_URL; first-login flow (seed an IAM access key via aws-cli, then log in); architecture diagram (browser → /console/ static → /admin/v1/ API → meta.Store + Prometheus); Phase 1 vs 2 vs 3 capability table
CLAUDE.md Big-picture architecture diagram updated: gateway box now reads 'S3 API + /console/ + /admin/v1/'
README.md How to run section gains a 6th option: open http://localhost:9000/console/ after make run-memory

Flip the ROADMAP P2 entry to Done close-flip format per CLAUDE.md 'Roadmap maintenance' rule: ~~**P2 — Web UI — Foundation (Phase 1).**~~ — **Done.** Embedded React+TS console served at /console/, login + cluster overview + nodes + top buckets + top consumers + metrics + buckets list + bucket detail. (commit `<sha>` or `(commit pending)`)
Add P3 entries pointing at Phase 2 (prd-web-ui-admin.md) and Phase 3 (prd-web-ui-debug.md) so the follow-ups are tracked
